title: cytisus procumbens var. macrophyllus
intro: Cytisus procumbens var. macrophyllus, also known as a variety of the procumbent broom, is a shrub-like plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and glycosides. The medicinal properties of Cytisus procumbens var. macrophyllus are believed to have health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and supporting digestive health, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. The plant is often used in herbal preparations like infusions, decoctions, and ointments, but its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
